steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has just described three patterns in how countries' numbers changed from 2009 to 2013: some increased, some decreased, and some stayed the same. The blank must continue the example by illustrating the "decreased" and "stayed the same" patterns, since the increase pattern was already covered as the contrasting case. Graph data: Philippines 5 (2009) $\Rightarrow$ 3 (2013) [decrease]; Belgium 6 (2009) $\Rightarrow$ 6 (2013) [same]; Guatemala 3 (2009) $\Rightarrow$ 4 (2013) [increase].

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the Philippines was lower in 2013 than in 2009, whereas the number in Belgium was the same in 2013 as in 2009.': Philippines decreased (5 $\Rightarrow$ 3) and Belgium stayed the same (6 $\Rightarrow$ 6) — directly illustrates the two patterns the passage is exemplifying. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Belgium was the same in 2013 as in 2009, but it had more women on its high courts than either Guatemala or the Philippines did in 2013.': Shifts to cross-country comparisons within a single year rather than changes over time — off-topic. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Guatemala was greater in 2013 than in 2009, whereas the number in the Philippines was lower in 2013 than in 2009.': Shows increase + decrease, but the passage already contrasts the other countries against the ones that increased — repeating increase doesn't fit. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Guatemala was greater in 2013 than in 2009, whereas the number in Belgium was the same in 2013 as in 2009.': Shows increase + same, but Guatemala increased, so it belongs to the group already described — it doesn't exemplify the decrease pattern. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
